seoTitle: Transfer Data From QuickBooks to Xero
metaDescription: How to export your QuickBooks financial data and import it into Xero, covering contacts, chart of accounts, and historical bills.
intro: Switching accounting platforms doesn't have to mean losing years of financial history or manually retyping thousands of transactions. This guide covers exporting your Chart of Accounts, customer and vendor lists, and transaction records from QuickBooks, then importing and verifying them inside Xero.

steps.heading: Export from QuickBooks
steps.body: Log into QuickBooks and download your Chart of Accounts and Customer/Vendor lists, then export historical transaction ledgers as Excel or CSV files.

steps.heading: Set your conversion date in Xero
steps.body: In Xero, go to Settings, then Advanced Settings, click Conversion Balances, and lock in your official platform switch date.

steps.heading: Import your contacts
steps.body: Go to the Contacts tab, click Import, and map your exported QuickBooks contact rows into Xero's format.

steps.heading: Upload your Chart of Accounts
steps.body: In Accounting Settings, click Chart of Accounts and upload your master account layout, resolving any tax code mismatches that come up.

steps.heading: Import historical bills and verify
steps.body: Upload your open accounts receivable and payable CSV rows, then run a trial balance report in both platforms to confirm the final balances match.

faq.question: What file format does Xero expect for imports?
faq.answer: Xero's import tools generally expect CSV spreadsheets, so exporting your QuickBooks data as Excel or CSV works well.

faq.question: Why do I need to set a conversion date?
faq.answer: The conversion date tells Xero exactly where your historical opening balances start, which keeps your reporting consistent between platforms.

faq.question: How do I know the migration was successful?
faq.answer: Run a fresh trial balance report in both QuickBooks and Xero and compare the final balances to confirm they match.

faq.question: Will tax codes transfer automatically?
faq.answer: Not always cleanly — you may need to review and resolve tax code mismatches manually during the Chart of Accounts import.
